diary:
Woke up in Bothell way too early, but not in a bad way—coffee first, then a quick tidy like I could straighten out the whole tax season just by wiping counters. I checked the schedule and, of course, the match time. Sunny Monday felt like it was daring me to get out of my own head. 

Stopped at Whole Foods on Roosevelt for a light breakfast to-go, then drove over toward Mercer Island. The Mercer Island Community Center and those school district offices have that familiar quiet, the kind where numbers start to feel like they’re clicking into place. By lunchtime I grabbed supplies at QFC, and the day kept moving. 

Then downtown Seattle—Pike Brewing in Pioneer Square was buzzing early for the USA vs Wales game. I watched with friends, ate something late and salty, and laughed way more than I expected. After, I walked the Waterfront Park for air and headed home, gear laid out for a hike tomorrow. I feel tired, but in a good, warmed-up way.
thought_process: I tried to keep the morning efficient since tax-season always punishes delays, so I started with the reconciliations early at the Mercer Island School District Administrative Offices and used the commute time to mentally map what needed filing vs. what could wait. Light rail being closed between Westlake and University District didn’t hit me much since I drove, but it still nudged me to plan downtown timing carefully so I wouldn’t get stuck near Pike Brewing. The World Cup match at 11am PDT was basically my reward system—something to look forward to once the office work was done. My mood shifted mid-afternoon: the paperwork tension eased as the day turned sunny and social. The night walk helped seal it; I went to bed feeling steadier than I started.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
